steps.title: Identify the exponential decay structure
steps.content:
The problem describes a decay process: start with 800 photons, and each 7 mm of material reduces the count by 50%. This is exponential decay of the form $I = I_0 \cdot b^n$, where:
  • $I_0$ = initial photons = 800
  • $b$ = decay factor per period = 0.5 (decreasing by 50% means keeping 50%)
  • $n$ = number of periods

steps.title: Set up the exponent based on distance
steps.content:
Since the decay factor applies once every 7 mm, and we are measuring distance $x$ millimeters from the surface, the number of complete 7 mm periods is $\frac{x}{7}$. Substituting into the exponential formula:
$$I = 800(0.5)^{\frac{x}{7}}$$
This matches choice D — D is correct.
